title: Add default /root if empty on chroot iso
body:
Checks to see if the $HOME envvar has been set
and if not, trys to set it as best as possible.

Fixes: #1592

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: @vrothberg thx for the test heads up.  I'm a bit surprised 'make' in my sandbox didn't catch that.....

Repushed with @rhatdan's homedir lookup changes.  As a root user, the userspec that's returned at the top of the GetUser() function appears to be empty and we're just defaulting all the time in that root.  I need to try as a non-root user in the morning.

@nalind @rhatdan I keep getting a compile error in Darwin that I'm not seeing in Travis or RedHat CI, nor my sandbox.  Any clues as to what I need to do to fix that?

```
### COMPLETED IN 0s
>>> cd $GOSRC/buildah && make darwin
GOOS=darwin go build -ldflags '-X main.GitCommit=03ce3cff -X main.buildInfo=1558263023 -X main.cniVersion=v0.7.0-rc2'  -o buildah.darwin -tags "containers_image_openpgp" ./cmd/buildah
# github.com/containers/buildah/pkg/chrootuser
pkg/chrootuser/user.go:68:18: undefined: lookupHomedirInContainer
make: *** [Makefile:39: darwin] Error 2
### EXITED WITH CODE 2 AFTER 6s
```

You need to add that function to

buildah/pkg/chrootuser/user_basic.go

As not supported.
@rhatdan thx, will see how that goes, have just added and repushed.

:pushpin: Commit 76ad226 has been approved by `rhatdan`

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
